hooks.command:
jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' | head -1 | grep -qE '^gh pr create ' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A pull request was just created. After reviewing the PR, you MUST also perform a README review.

## README Review Checklist

Read the full README.md and the PR diff. Check each item below and fix any drift or gaps directly (do NOT ask — just fix and commit, or confirm it is current):

### Structure (compare against uv, Starship, ripgrep, fzf, Zoxide)
1. One-line description immediately after logo — clear, jargon-free
2. Badges: 4-6 max, ordered Release > CI > Platform > License. Remove stale or broken badges. Use consistent style (flat-square). All badges must link somewhere.
3. Quick nav links if project has docs site
4. Visual demo (GIF, screenshot, or terminal output) above the fold
5. Feature highlights: 4-8 scannable bullets with bold keywords
6. Installation front and center — separate code blocks per platform, numbered steps
7. Usage examples — real console sessions showing input+output (show, dont tell)
8. Collapsible <details> for multi-platform installs and optional features
9. Development/Contributing section — welcoming, links to guide
10. License at the bottom

### Content accuracy (the most important check)
- Project structure diagram matches actual directory layout
- All commands shown in examples actually work
- Version references are not hardcoded to stale values
- Install instructions match the current release mechanism
- Module/feature descriptions match current code
- No removed features still documented, no new features undocumented
- Requirements section matches actual platform support

### Quality signals
- GitHub admonitions ([!NOTE], [!WARNING], [!TIP]) for prerequisites, caveats, alpha status
- Dark/light mode responsive images via <picture> tags
- No badge walls (>6), no vanity badges, no stale CI links
- Table of Contents if README exceeds 4 screenfuls
- Upgrade instructions if relevant

If the README is already accurate and follows best practices, say so briefly and move on."}}' || true
